tips.tip: Use sub-agents for exploration. Every file Claude reads yourself costs tokens. Dispatch the explore agent instead.
tips.example: Say: 'Dispatch explore agent to find where the auth logic lives'

tips.tip: Read specific line ranges, not whole files. Use offset and limit parameters: Read(file, offset=50, limit=30)
tips.example: Claude reads lines 50-80 instead of all 500 lines

tips.tip: Run /compact when the conversation gets long. Claude loses focus past ~80% context. Compact resets the window.
tips.example: Type /compact in Claude Code to compress the conversation

tips.tip: Start a new session for each major task. Carrying context from a previous task wastes tokens on irrelevant history.
tips.example: Finish the auth feature → commit → start fresh session for the dashboard

tips.tip: Use /plan before coding. A task breakdown prevents wasted work and keeps Claude on the critical path.
tips.example: Say: '/plan: add Stripe checkout to the pricing page'

tips.tip: Commit often. Small commits make it easy to undo mistakes and keep Claude's context focused.
tips.example: Commit after each feature, not at the end of the day

tips.tip: Run /review before every PR. Claude catches bugs in 2 minutes that would take hours to debug in production.
tips.example: Say: '/review' before git push origin

tips.tip: Keep SESSION_STATE.md updated. It lets Claude resume exactly where you left off after a break.
tips.example: Claude updates it automatically after each checkpoint hook

tips.tip: Tell Claude what NOT to change. It's as important as what to change.
tips.example: Say: 'Add the feature but don't touch the auth middleware'

tips.tip: Ask Claude to explain what it just did. Understanding builds trust and catches mistakes early.
tips.example: Say: 'Walk me through what you just changed and why'

tips.tip: Run tests before marking any task done. The validate hook does this automatically if configured.
tips.example: Say: '/test' after every implementation task

tips.tip: Use /security before deploying. A 3-minute scan beats a 3-hour incident response.
tips.example: Say: '/security' before any production push

tips.tip: Write tests before fixing bugs (TDD). Claude is much better at fixing bugs when there's a failing test to guide it.
tips.example: Say: 'Write a failing test for this bug first, then fix it'

tips.tip: Ask Claude to review its own plan before executing. Catching a bad plan early is free.
tips.example: Say: 'Before you start, what could go wrong with this approach?'

tips.tip: Use git blame to understand WHY code exists before changing it. Context prevents regressions.
tips.example: Ask Claude: 'Why does this code look like this? Check git blame'

tips.tip: Describe outcomes, not steps. 'Users should be able to log in with Google' is better than 'Add OAuth'.
tips.example: Say what the user should experience, not the implementation

tips.tip: Give Claude examples when the format matters. 'Like X but for Y' is powerful.
tips.example: Say: 'A card like the Stripe dashboard's recent transactions'

tips.tip: Reference specific files when asking about code. 'In src/api/users.ts' is clearer than 'in the users file'.
tips.example: Say: 'In src/api/users.ts around line 42, why is this null-checked?'

tips.tip: Say 'ask me if unclear' to prevent Claude from guessing on ambiguous requirements.
tips.example: Add to any prompt: '...and ask me if anything is unclear before starting'

tips.tip: Use CLAUDE.md for persistent rules. Anything you find yourself saying every session belongs there.
tips.example: Add: 'Always use pnpm, never npm' to CLAUDE.md

tips.tip: Add the explore agent first if you have a large codebase. It pays for itself on the first use.
tips.example: Dispatch it once to build a mental model, then use targeted reads

tips.tip: Configure the block-dangerous hook before doing anything else. It's insurance that costs nothing.
tips.example: Takes 2 minutes to set up; prevents accidental force pushes to main

tips.tip: Add your deployment target to CLAUDE.md. Claude can then run /deploy without asking every time.
tips.example: Add: 'Deploy target: Vercel, project: my-app'

tips.tip: Update USER_PROFILE.json if your role or goals change. It affects which templates and agents Claude uses.
tips.example: Say: 'Update my profile: I'm now leading a team of 3'

tips.tip: Use parallel agents for independent tasks. Two agents working simultaneously cuts time in half.
tips.example: Say: 'Dispatch two agents: one to write tests, one to write docs'

tips.tip: End each session with a summary. Claude can write SESSION_STATE.md for you in 30 seconds.
tips.example: Say: 'Update SESSION_STATE.md with what we accomplished today'

tips.tip: Give Claude your git workflow preferences once, in CLAUDE.md. Never repeat 'use conventional commits' again.
tips.example: Add: 'Commit format: feat(scope): description — conventional commits'

tips.tip: Use worktrees for parallel features. Git worktrees let you work on two branches simultaneously without stashing.
tips.example: Say: 'Create a worktree for feat/payments so we can work in parallel'

tips.tip: Never put secrets in CLAUDE.md or any committed file. Use environment variables.
tips.example: process.env.API_KEY not 'sk-abc123' in code

tips.tip: Review Claude's changes before committing, especially in auth, payments, and database code.
tips.example: Run: git diff --staged before every git commit
